steps.title: Recognize the minimum occurs at the vertex
steps.content: Since $y = x^2 - 14x + 22$ is a quadratic with $a = 1 \gt  0$, the parabola opens upward and has a minimum value at its vertex. The x-coordinate of the vertex is found using this key formula:

steps.title: Apply the vertex formula to find x
steps.content:
From the equation, identify $a = 1$ and $b = -14$. Substitute into the formula:
$$x = -\frac{(-14)}{2(1)} = \frac{14}{2} = 7$$
The minimum value of $y$ occurs at $x = 7$.
